name: Mexican Vegetable Part:
recipeIngredient: 1 pint Tomatoes (I used the tomatoes my mom and I canned last summer)
3/4 cup Onions, sliced how you like them
1/2 cup Reduced-fat Monterey Jack or Cheddar cheese, or some combo thereof
recipeInstructions:
1. Saute the onions and pepper in a large skillet over medium heat, just until soft.
2. Stir in the rest of the vegetable ingredients; simmer 15 minutes or so. Salt and pepper to taste.
3. In a mixing bowl, combine cornmeal crust ingredients; whisk briefly.
4. Turn the mixture into a vegetable-sprayed pie dish.
5. Bake at 475°F for 10 minutes or until puffy and brown.
6. Add the vegetable mixture, then sprinkle cheese on top.
7. Bake for ten more minutes or so, or just until the cheese is melted to your liking.
